qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Valid pickle opcodes crash Fickling's default parser and safety-check paths
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes uncaught NotImplementedError exceptions in Fickling's parser and CLI when valid but unsupported opcodes such as FLOAT reach Pickled.load()/fickling.loads(). The advisory instead describes incomplete monkey-patching in always_check_safety()/run_hook() that leaves pickle.loads, _pickle.loads, and _pickle.load unhooked, so it is a different vulnerable code path and root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's hook-coverage bypass in always_check_safety()/run_hook(). The only real qualifying finding is a separate denial-of-service condition where valid but unsupported pickle opcodes crash Fickling's own parser and CLI with uncaught NotImplementedError.
evidence: The advisory summary and details describe missing safety hooks for pickle.loads, _pickle.loads, and _pickle.load, allowing malicious pickle execution despite always_check_safety() being enabled.
The session manifest lists exactly one final qualifying finding: valid_pickle_opcode_dos.
That finding report attributes the bug to missing opcode implementations in repo/fickling/fickle.py and to uncaught NotImplementedError reaching library and CLI parsing paths.

The finding never identifies always_check_safety(), run_hook(), or unhooked stdlib pickle entrypoints, so it does not describe the advisory root cause or an equivalent source-to-sink/control-flow condition.
